summary: New Jersey's official AI-in-education footprint is real but lighter-touch than states with a formal statewide guidance document. NJDOE maintains an official AI resource hub and, in 2025, issued a statewide grant program to support AI classroom pilots, curricula, and teacher training. The evidence supports an emerging-to-framework stage rather than a fully formalized statewide policy.

candidate_sources.title: Artificial Intelligence (AI) | New Jersey Department of Education
candidate_sources.why_it_matters: This is the main official NJDOE AI page and frames the state's AI-in-education posture.

candidate_sources.title: New Jersey Department of Education Announces Grant Awards to Support Artificial Intelligence in Education
candidate_sources.why_it_matters: The grant announcement provides the clearest official evidence of active implementation, teacher training, and AI classroom pilots.

ai_use_allowed.rationale: NJDOE signals that AI should be discussed and responsibly explored, and the 2025 grants explicitly back local classroom pilots, but the state does not yet publish a stronger statewide classroom-use rule set.
ai_use_allowed.evidence_quote: This webpage provides an overview of foundational AI terms and concepts, high-level discussion questions regarding AI in education to consider at the Local Education Agency (LEA) leadership and classroom educator levels...

assessment_policy.rationale: The reviewed official sources do not contain explicit statewide AI assessment or disclosure rules.

privacy_policy.rationale: The reviewed official sources do not contain explicit statewide AI student-data privacy or procurement controls.

teacher_pd_support.rationale: NJDOE is materially supporting educator learning through official webinars, curated educator resources, and grants that include teacher training in AI integration.
teacher_pd_support.evidence_quote: The “Teaching with AI” component will support initiatives such as pilot programs using generative AI tools to enhance instructional practices... and teacher training in AI integration.

implementation_stage.rationale: New Jersey is beyond simple exploration because statewide grant funding is backing local AI pilots and AI curricula, but the evidence still stops short of a full statewide formal policy framework.
implementation_stage.evidence_quote: The “Artificial Intelligence Innovation in Education Grant” will support initiatives in 10 school districts to advance the role of AI in the classroom.

open_questions: If NJDOE publishes a formal statewide AI guidance memo or model policy, the state's implementation-stage score should likely increase.
Privacy and assessment coding should remain zero until explicit statewide language appears.

approval_recommendation.rationale: New Jersey now has supportable evidence for teacher-support and implementation activity, but the current posture is still more resource-and-grant oriented than formal policy, so sample audit remains the right route.

recommended_record.notes: Deep-research review confirms that New Jersey has an official NJDOE AI resource hub and active statewide AI grantmaking for local pilots and teacher training, but not yet a stronger formal statewide AI guidance document.
recommended_record.verification_notes: Official New Jersey sources support low-to-moderate AI use, teacher-support, and implementation coding. Assessment and privacy remain uncoded because the reviewed state sources are not explicit on those domains.

source_documents.title: Artificial Intelligence (AI) | New Jersey Department of Education
New Jersey Department of Education Announces Grant Awards to Support Artificial Intelligence in Education

evidence_spans.quote: This webpage provides an overview of foundational AI terms and concepts, high-level discussion questions regarding AI in education to consider at the Local Education Agency (LEA) leadership and classroom educator levels...
The “Teaching with AI” component will support initiatives such as pilot programs using generative AI tools to enhance instructional practices... and teacher training in AI integration.
The “Artificial Intelligence Innovation in Education Grant” will support initiatives in 10 school districts to advance the role of AI in the classroom.

gold_record.notes: Deep-research-reviewed hard case. Official New Jersey sources support an emerging, grant-backed AI posture rather than a full statewide formal policy.
